stat: Phil Taylor has hit 11 confirmed nine-dart finishes in professional competition — more than any other player in history. Gary Anderson is second with just 2.
share_text: Did you know? Phil Taylor hit 11 nine-dart finishes in his career — more than anyone else in history. #DartsDaily

stat: Phil Taylor won 16 World Championship titles during his career — a record that may never be broken in any sport.
share_text: Phil Taylor won 16 World Championship titles. A record that may never be broken. #DartsDaily

stat: Michael van Gerwen holds the record for the highest ever televised three-dart average — 123.40 against Adrian Lewis in the 2016 Premier League.
share_text: MvG holds the record for the highest ever televised average — 123.40. Utterly ridiculous. #DartsDaily

stat: A standard game of darts starts at 501 and must be finished on a double. The minimum number of darts needed to achieve this is just 9.
share_text: A game of darts starts at 501. The minimum darts needed to win? Just 9. #DartsDaily

stat: John Lowe won £102,000 for hitting the first ever televised nine-dart finish in 1984 — a record prize at the time that made headlines worldwide.
share_text: John Lowe won £102,000 for the first ever televised nine-darter in 1984. #DartsDaily

stat: Michael van Gerwen has won the Premier League Darts title a record 7 times — more than any other player in the tournament's history.
share_text: MvG has won the Premier League Darts title 7 times. Nobody else comes close. #DartsDaily

stat: Luke Littler became PDC World Champion at just 17 years old — making him the youngest world champion in the history of the PDC.
share_text: Luke Littler became PDC World Champion at just 17 years old. The future is now. #DartsDaily

stat: Phil Taylor went on a 72-match unbeaten run in 2001 — one of the most dominant stretches of form ever seen in professional darts.
share_text: Phil Taylor went 72 matches unbeaten in 2001. One of sport's greatest ever runs of form. #DartsDaily

stat: Eric Bristow won 5 World Championship titles between 1980 and 1986 — dominating an entire decade of darts like no player before him.
share_text: Eric Bristow won 5 World titles between 1980 and 1986. He owned the decade. #DartsDaily

stat: John Part won World Championship titles across three different decades — 1994, 2003 and 2008 — a feat of longevity unmatched in the sport.
share_text: John Part won World titles in 3 different decades. One of darts greatest achievements. #DartsDaily

stat: The maximum checkout in darts is 170 — Triple 20, Triple 20, Bullseye. It is the holy grail of finishing combinations.
share_text: The maximum checkout in darts is 170. Triple 20, Triple 20, Bullseye. Pure perfection. #DartsDaily

stat: Martin Adams won his first BDO World Championship title at the age of 50 — making him one of the oldest first-time world champions in darts history.
share_text: Martin Adams won his first World title at 50 years old. Proof it is never too late. #DartsDaily

stat: Michael van Gerwen has hit 9 televised nine-dart finishes in his career — second only to Phil Taylor's record of 11.
share_text: MvG has hit 9 televised nine-darters. Only Phil Taylor has hit more. #DartsDaily

stat: The first ever televised nine-dart finish was thrown by John Lowe on 13 October 1984 at the MFI World Matchplay — a moment that changed darts forever.
share_text: The first televised nine-dart finish was in 1984. John Lowe. A moment that changed darts forever. #DartsDaily

stat: The maximum score with a single dart is 60 — Triple 20. A perfect leg requires hitting Triple 20 six times among other scoring combinations.
share_text: The max score with one dart is 60 — Triple 20. Six of those in a leg and you are flying. #DartsDaily

stat: The PDC World Championship at Alexandra Palace now offers a total prize fund of £500,000 — compared to just £8,000 when the tournament began in 1994.
share_text: The PDC World Championship prize fund has grown from £8,000 in 1994 to £500,000 today. Darts has come a long way. #DartsDaily

stat: In 2019, Fallon Sherrock became the first woman to beat a man at the PDC World Championship — a historic moment watched by millions worldwide.
share_text: In 2019 Fallon Sherrock became the first woman to beat a man at the PDC Worlds. A moment in sporting history. #DartsDaily

stat: The PDC World Championship features 96 players competing across 32 days at Alexandra Palace — making it one of the longest running sporting events of the year.
share_text: The PDC World Darts Championship runs for 32 days at Alexandra Palace. One of sport's longest annual events. #DartsDaily

stat: Phil Taylor won 6 consecutive PDC World Championship titles between 1995 and 2002 — a run of dominance that has never been matched in the sport.
share_text: Phil Taylor won 6 consecutive World titles between 1995 and 2002. The most dominant run in darts history. #DartsDaily

stat: The bullseye scores 50 points and the outer bull scores 25. Most players aim for Triple 20 as their primary scoring target rather than the bull.
share_text: Fun darts fact: most pros aim for Triple 20, not the bullseye, as their primary scoring target. #DartsDaily

stat: The prize for hitting a nine-dart finish at the PDC World Championship is £10,000 on top of any match winnings — a bonus that every player dreams of claiming.
share_text: Hit a nine-darter at the PDC World Championship and you win an extra £10,000. Every player's dream. #DartsDaily

stat: The PDC was founded in 1993 when 16 top players broke away from the BDO — a split that transformed darts into the professional sport it is today.
share_text: The PDC was founded in 1993 by 16 players who broke away from the BDO. That decision changed darts forever. #DartsDaily

stat: The official throwing distance in darts is 2.37 metres from the front of the board to the oche — a measurement standardised across all professional tournaments worldwide.
share_text: The official throwing distance in darts is 2.37 metres. Standardised worldwide for every professional tournament. #DartsDaily

stat: Glen Durrant won 3 consecutive BDO World Championship titles from 2017 to 2019 — one of the most dominant runs of form in BDO history.
share_text: Glen Durrant won 3 consecutive BDO World titles from 2017 to 2019. One of darts most dominant ever runs. #DartsDaily

stat: The centre of a dartboard — the bullseye — must be mounted exactly 1.73 metres from the floor in all professional tournaments. This is standardised worldwide.
share_text: The bullseye must be exactly 1.73 metres from the floor in every professional darts tournament worldwide. #DartsDaily

stat: Tony O'Shea reached the BDO World Championship final at the age of 53 — making him one of the oldest finalists in the history of any World Darts Championship.
share_text: Tony O'Shea reached the BDO World Championship final at age 53. Proof that darts has no age limit. #DartsDaily

stat: John Part became the first non-European player to win a World Darts Championship when he claimed the BDO title in 1994 — putting Canada on the global darts map.
share_text: In 1994 John Part became the first non-European World Darts Champion. Canada on the global darts map. #DartsDaily

stat: There are 20 numbered sections on a standard dartboard, ranging from 1 to 20. The layout is specifically designed so that high numbers are surrounded by low numbers to penalise inaccuracy.
share_text: A dartboard has 20 sections. The layout is designed to punish inaccuracy by surrounding high numbers with low ones. #DartsDaily

stat: The total viewing figures for the 2024 PDC World Championship final peaked at over 4.5 million in the UK alone — the highest for any darts broadcast in years.
share_text: The 2024 PDC World Championship final peaked at 4.5 million viewers in the UK. Darts is bigger than ever. #DartsDaily

stat: A maximum break in snooker is called 147. The darts equivalent — a nine-dart finish — is statistically far rarer to hit under professional match pressure.
share_text: A nine-dart finish in darts is statistically rarer under match pressure than a 147 in snooker. Mind blowing. #DartsDaily
